# UC-22.63.3 · IEC 61511 Clause 11.7.6 SIS-BPCS separation + SIS override and bypass logging with audit-trail tamper-detection

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Anomaly &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Sometimes operators must temporarily turn off part of a safety system to do maintenance. We track every time this happens, make sure the right people approved it, that there is a written reason and time limit, and that it is turned back on within the allowed time.*

---

## Description

Reconciles every SIS override / bypass event against the IEC 61511-1 Clause 11.7.6 discipline: (i) authorisation by a procedure-authorised operator, (ii) business-justification recorded, (iii) work-permit reference linked, (iv) duration within the procedure-mandated maximum, and (v) override closed at the end of the authorised window. Critical conditions: an override without an authorising operator (risk_score=100), or an override still open past its authorised duration (risk_score=100 — operator-discipline breach with direct safety-function-impairment risk).

## Value

IEC 61511 Clause 11.7.6 is the single most commonly cited finding in process-safety regulator audits (OSHA PSM CCNs, COMAH inspector reports, Seveso enforcement notices). 'Operators leave overrides engaged for shifts at a time' is the canonical post-incident finding. The audit-trail produced by this UC defends the operating organisation in a regulator-investigation and provides leading-indicator metrics to the Plant Safety Manager (overrides-engaged-time per shift, escalation-rate to shift supervisor) that correlate strongly with safety performance.

## Implementation

(1) Wire the SIS logic-solver to Splunk via vendor TA (Siemens F-CPU TA / Honeywell SM TA / HIMA HIMax TA / Emerson DeltaV SIS TA / Yokogawa ProSafe-RS TA). (2) Maintain `iec_61511_sis_override_procedure_lookup` with per-SIF maximum-authorised durations (typically 60 min for in-shift, 480 min for shift-spanning with shift-supervisor escalation). (3) Schedule UC-22.63.3 every 30 minutes; route `risk_score = 100` (open-overrun) to Shift Supervisor + Functional Safety Manager at severity P1, paging.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.63.3` archived hourly to `audit_evidence` with `iec_clause="IEC-61511-Cl-11-7-6"`. Every override / bypass / write-to-SIS event pins (sis_id, event_type, operator_id, authorised_by, business_justification, duration_authorised_min, duration_actual_min, audit_evidence_ref, risk_score).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

An operator places a SIF in override for 60 minutes authorised but leaves the override engaged for 300 minutes without a re-authorisation. UC-22.63.3 MUST flag with risk_score=95 once the authorised window expires.

### Negative scenario

An operator places the SIF in override with a documented work-permit reference, the authorisation is signed by the shift supervisor, and the override is cleared within the authorised 60-minute window with a closure note. UC-22.63.3 MUST record risk_score=0.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- A vendor-specific TA for the deployed SIS logic-solver platform (Siemens / Honeywell / HIMA / Emerson / Yokogawa / Schneider).
- An operator-authorisation system (typically the SIS engineering workstation or a paper / electronic permit-to-work system).

## Step 1 - Override-procedure KV Store

```ini
[iec_61511_sis_override_procedure_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.sis_id = string
field.sif_id = string
field.procedure_ref = string
field.max_authorised_duration_min = number
field.escalation_authority = string
```

Seed typical entries:

```
sis_id,sif_id,procedure_ref,max_authorised_duration_min,escalation_authority
SIS-CRACKER-A,SIF-HHL-1,PROC-001-A,60,Shift-Supervisor
SIS-CRACKER-A,SIF-HPHL-2,PROC-001-A,30,Plant-Manager
SIS-COMPRESSOR-B,SIF-VIB-1,PROC-002-B,120,Shift-Supervisor
```

## Step 2 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.63.3: SIS Override and Bypass Register]
cron_schedule = */30 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -2h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.63.3,reg=IEC-61511,clause=IEC-61511-Cl-11-7-6
alert.severity = 5
```

## Step 3 - Functional-safety-specific notes

- **Force counters**: many SIS platforms (Siemens F-CPU, HIMA) expose 'force counters' as Modbus tags — surface these as `sis:force_counter` for engineering verification.
- **Plant-Manager escalation**: for SIL-3 SIF overrides, the escalation_authority should typically be the Plant Manager, not the Shift Supervisor — calibrate per the plant's safety-case.

## SPL

```spl
index=ot_sis sourcetype IN ("sis:override","sis:bypass","sis:logic_solver") event_type IN ("override_start","override_end","bypass_start","bypass_end")
| transaction sis_id sif_id maxspan=24h startswith=eval(event_type IN ("override_start","bypass_start")) endswith=eval(event_type IN ("override_end","bypass_end"))
| eval duration_actual_min = duration / 60
| eval still_open = if(eventcount = 1, 1, 0)
| lookup iec_61511_sis_override_procedure_lookup sis_id, sif_id OUTPUT procedure_ref, max_authorised_duration_min, escalation_authority
| eval authorisation_present = if(isnotnull(authorised_by) AND authorised_by != "", 1, 0)
| eval justification_present = if(isnotnull(business_justification) AND business_justification != "", 1, 0)
| eval permit_ref_present = if(isnotnull(work_permit_ref) AND work_permit_ref != "", 1, 0)
| eval duration_overrun = if(isnotnull(duration_actual_min) AND duration_actual_min > max_authorised_duration_min, 1, 0)
| eval procedure_missing = if(isnull(procedure_ref), 1, 0)
| eval still_open_overrun = if(still_open = 1 AND ((now() - _time) / 60) > max_authorised_duration_min, 1, 0)
| eval risk_score = case(
    authorisation_present = 0, 100,
    still_open_overrun = 1, 100,
    duration_overrun = 1, 95,
    justification_present = 0, 85,
    permit_ref_present = 0, 75,
    procedure_missing = 1, 70,
    still_open = 1, 30,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| sort 0 -risk_score, -duration_actual_min
| table sis_id sif_id event_type operator_id authorised_by business_justification work_permit_ref duration_actual_min max_authorised_duration_min still_open risk_score
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Live open-override register with elapsed time. (Panel 2) Override-duration distribution. (Panel 3) Operator-discipline trend by team. (Panel 4) Procedure-coverage status.

## Known False Positives

1. **Boot / startup transient**: a logic-solver reboot can briefly register override-start without a corresponding operator action — suppress with `boot_time_within_300s`. 2. **Proof-test bypass (UC-22.63.4)**: a bypass that is part of a documented proof-test procedure — load `proof_test_ref` and exclude from this UC's pipeline; UC-22.63.4 monitors proof-tests. 3. **Operator-on-leave handoff**: a long-running override that crosses shifts may be re-authorised by the incoming shift's supervisor — load `re_authorisation_chain_ref`.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `iec_61511_22_63_3_exceptions` with the Functional Safety Manager + Plant Manager documented approval, an explicit sis_id + sif_id + time-window scope, an expiry date, and a written justification.

## References

- [IEC 61511-1:2016 Clause 11.7.6 — Bypasses, overrides, maintenance forces](https://webstore.iec.ch/publication/24241)
- [IEC 61511-1:2016 Clause 11.2.10 — Separation of SIS from BPCS](https://webstore.iec.ch/publication/24241)
- [OSHA Process Safety Management standard 29 CFR 1910.119](https://www.osha.gov/laws-regs/regulations/standardnumber/1910/1910.119)
